sast_review.reason: This is a generic informational warning about importing the subprocess module. The code does not use shell=True or any dangerous subprocess feature; the import itself is not a security vulnerability. The actual usage is safe as shown by the validated command list.

subprocess.run is called with a list (cmd) and no shell=True, so no shell injection is possible. The image name is validated via regex allowing only safe characters. Environment variable names are validated, and values are passed as part of a single list element ('-e', 'NAME=VALUE'), which prevents injection even if the value contains special characters. The command is fully under the developer's control after validation.

raw.summary: Both SAST findings are false positives. bandit:B404 is a generic import warning with no actual vulnerability. bandit:B603 flags subprocess.run but the command list is constructed from validated inputs (image regex-validated, env names validated, values passed as single argument) and shell=False is the default, so no command injection is possible.
